reviews.review_description: It's really fun but the game has its fair share of bugs and frustrating moments. But if you just stick a little annoyance through it's over all a fun thing. Also having to do some of the most important game stuff on my phone and keep having to take my headset on and off is very annoying. Fun game if you like D&D and it's worth checking out!

Needs free movement and turning instead of teleport and snap, more customization for coloring your avatar, and would like to be able to have telechonises for objects that are unreachable but are usable. I still don't understand how to play yet since the tutorial gives no instructions.
I've played the early access and I was pretty disappointed with the bugs, but now I can say that the bugs are all gone and the menus have a new slicker feeling UI.

As someone who watched this game grow from 2020, this game came a long way. The game allows you to create anything you want and build 3d maps. The community is very supportive too and its a great place to learn how to play. All this being develop by one guy too.
Exactly what I wanted for those day I wanna play dnd in person but cannot.

reviews.review_title: A little buggy but still great
reviews.review_description: Not much to say here. I have been playing this with a group for the past few days staying up until like 2am. I’ve had a blast with the system even though the dice are definitely cursed lol. Overall great game tell your friends to get it.

Join tavertales discord ask to join sakara in the (#looking for a group) section. We run games almost everyday. Our western March style campaign has hundreds of players and GMs if you love playing D&D or have always wanted to play this is the game for you its like having a giant table to play on in your basement with monster minis trees water castles. MAKE FULL 3D WORLD'S that you can run your games on. This game is great so glad I found it.
Dead game with no players on it

This game has been a game changer for me. Going through college I would always play D&D with a group of friends, then we all graduated and went our separate ways. I've never been huge into roll20 as I really struggle to feel immersed in the game. Enter Tavern Tales.
The controls aren't super intuitive, but I don't know that you can make intuitive controls and have all the functionality necessary. 5-10 minutes in and you're all set though. It's got great features that makes DMing WAY easier than being in person. The game still has a bug here and there, but the developer is actively working on it and is always improving.
Map building is fun and amazing and you have all the tools you need to create an immersive game of D&D and still have the feel of being at a table with your friends.

This is by far the best VR tabletop game developed. And I can only imagine how it will escalate from here. It's a lot of fun to be able to create and play dnd RPG campaigns in 360 VR. Technology is on par with our imaginations and that's very exciting. I'd enjoy a more realistic set up but the pixelated set up is cool too. Great job on this app. If you like tabletop gaming your not gonna be disappointed with this app.
